content: Just as the educational linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive step to connect surface language input to deeper conceptual understanding, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can be enhanced by drawing on LLM multiple-perspective self-reflection mechanisms to build a hierarchical dynamic memory that separates surface-level reflection on action outputs from deeper conceptual reflection on core reasoning patterns. This hybrid structure addresses a key limitation of current LLM self-reflection that often only revises outputs without re-examining the foundational assumptions that generated flawed outputs in the first place.
